name: ftfy (Fix Text For You - Unicode Repair)

performance.notes: Pure Python; very fast for typical text sizes

agent_readiness.notes: Fix mojibake and Unicode encoding errors in text. import ftfy. ftfy.fix_text('â€œHello, world!â€\x9d'). ftfy.fix_encoding('SchÃ¶n'). ftfy.fix_text(text, normalization='NFC'). ftfy.explain_unicode('â€œ'). ftfy.fix_and_explain(text). Common use: scraped web data, PDFs with encoding issues, text imported from Windows systems. Fixes: mojibake, multiple encodings, HTML entities, Unicode normalization, byte order marks. Does NOT: translate, fix spelling, or fix punctuation.

gotchas: Can over-correct — sometimes valid non-ASCII text is misidentified as mojibake
Not a general text cleaning tool — only fixes encoding issues
ftfy.fix_text() applies heuristics — review output for domain-specific text
Does not fix HTML/XML content (use html.unescape first)
best_when: Cleaning web-scraped text with mojibake (encoding corruption)
Fixing text from legacy Windows encodings (Windows-1252 → UTF-8)
Pre-processing scraped/imported text datasets before NLP
avoid_when: When input encoding is already known and correct
summary: ftfy fixes Unicode encoding corruption (mojibake) in text. ftfy.fix_text('â€œHello world â€\x9d'). fix_encoding(). explain_unicode(). fix_and_explain(). Apache-2.0 by Robyn Speer/Luminoso. Essential for cleaning web-scraped or legacy-imported text. Idempotent and fast. Can over-correct — review output for specialized text. Not a general text cleaner.
